site.name: CMSBar Nuxt example
site.description: A site server-rendered by Nuxt 3 with the framework-neutral CMS API mounted as one Nitro catch-all route.

home.title: CMSBar runs in this Nuxt host
home.description: A site server-rendered by Nuxt 3, with the framework-neutral CMSBar API mounted as one catch-all Nitro route.

hero.headline: Click anything. Change it. Ship it as a PR.
hero.subtitle: This text is editable - log in and click it.

demo.title: CMSBar runs in this Nuxt host
demo.intro: This heading and paragraph are server-rendered by Nuxt 3 from the framework-neutral content model. pages/index.vue reads getContent() on the server during SSR and renders it - so the content is in the HTML, not assembled by client JS. The whole CMSBar API is mounted at /api/cms/* through a single Nitro catch-all route that forwards the raw web Request to createCmsApi().
demo.body:
Editable in place, no React

This block renders real content from the model. Log in via the bar below and it becomes a contenteditable rich-text region - the whole CMSBar UI (bar, editable primitives, drawers) is native Vue 3 over the framework-neutral lib/cmsbar core.
